title: Rights of Sentience
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Citation}}
The '''Rights of Sentience''' was a clause of the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Galactic Constitution]]. It spoke out [[Anti-speciesism|against]] [[speciesism]] and set out the rights that all members of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] were entitled to, including these:

*The formal outlawing of [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]].
*The declaration that all sentient lifeforms were equal, and should be treated as such.
*All Republic citizens were entitled to all rights enshrined in the Constitution, including suffrage, protection from undue hardships, etc.

When the Galactic Constitution was written in [[25,053 BBY]], the Rights of Sentience was one of its most radical sections. However, throughout its history, the Republic was hard-pressed to enforce the Rights, especially in its outlying regions. During the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the Galactic Constitution was removed and replaced by the [[Imperial Charter/Legends|Imperial Charter]] which promoted [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]], but the clause were still practiced by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in defiance of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and was reinstated it when the [[New Republic Common Charter]] was drafted, restoring the Galactic Constitution under the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. [[New Republic Senate Resolution 54.325|Senate Resolution 54.325]] helped enforce the Rights of Sentience. 

The development of this clause stirred strong emotions among the [[Core Founder/Legends|founders]], with one [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] declaring that he would rather die than allow others to be subjected to slavery, citing the suffering it had inflicted on his ancestors during the era of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] and its near destruction of his bloodline.<ref>[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia|''Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia'']]</ref> 

It is not known how the Galactic Republic determined which species were sentient. Such proved a problem for the [[Droid rights/Legends|droid rights movement]], as [[Sentience programming|droid sentience]] was questionable at best.

==Appearances==
*''[[Cloak of Deception]]''
*[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']] {{1stm}}
*''[[Planet of Twilight]]''
